ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-30-2010 IN 206, DEFENDANT SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC USA INC'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER RE: PLAINTIFFS' EXCESSIVE DISCOVERY. CASSIANA AARONSON APPEARED FOR PLAINTIFFS. ALICE AHN APPEARED FOR DEFENDANT SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC USA INC. HAVING NO OPPOSITION ON FILE BY PLAINTIFFS, THE COURT TOOK A HALF HOUR RECESS FOR PLAINTIFFS TO FILE THEIR OPPOSITION. THE MATTER WAS RECALLED AND MISS AARONSON REPRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT THE OPPOSITION HAS BEEN E-FILED AND SERVED AND THE DEFENDANT HAS NO OBJECTION ON PROCEEDING ON THE MERITS. AFTER ARGUMENT, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER WAS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS STATED ON THE RECORD. THE COURT MAKES A SPECIFIC FINDING THAT THE PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO MEET AND CONFER AT ALL IN GOOD FAITH AND HAVE UNSUCCESSFULLY OPPOSED THE MOTION. THE RULINGS MADE GRANTING OR OVERRULING THE OBJECTIONS ON THE PROTECTIVE ORDER ARE SUBJECT TO WAIVER ON THE THEORY THAT DEFENDANT HAS RESPONDED TO SOME OR ALL OF THE DISCOVERY TO WHICH THE COURT HAS SUSTAINED THE OBJECTION AND GRANTED THE PROTECTIVE ORDER. IT WILL BE SPECIFICALLY LAID OUT IN THE ORDER THAT ALTHOUGH THE ORDER WAS GRANTED PRIOR TO THE HEARING, THE DEFENDANT WAIVED. THE ORDER WILL ALSO INCLUDE SAVING LANGUAGE THAT THE PORTIONS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE DEFENDANT'S RIGHT TO RENEW A MOTION ON SEPARATE, DIFFERENT, NOT THE SAME DISCOVERY DEMANDS THAN THOSE SPECIFICALLY ITEMIZED. PER DIEM FEES HAVE BEEN ASSESSED PURSUANT TO G.C. 68086 FF AND CRC 2.958. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R. VELUZ; COURT REPORTER: RITA R. LERNER, CSR# 3179 [818-609-9803], PRESENT FOR PLAINTIFF. (206)

Zachariah D. Baker of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8034), appearing for defendant / moving party Schneider Electric USA, Inc. FKA Square D Company
Wes Wagnon of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs
/ opposing parties Charles Luce, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC USA, INC. FKA SQUARE D COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc. FKA Square D Company's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden on summary judgment and all issues of summary adjudication. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting her claims against defendant. By failing to submit with its moving papers verified factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support her claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's objections on the grounds of defendant's failure to submit verifications are sustained. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.). Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT ERICSSON, INC., INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO ANACONDA WIRE & CABLE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ericsson Inc., Individually and as Successor-In-Interest To Anaconda Wire & Cable Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Luce was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Even if Mr. Kothe's declaration is admissible, when read in conjunction with Mr. Luce's deposition testimony it merely creates a triable issue. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Sara K. Egide of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST LLP (415-617-2209), appearing for defendant / moving party Rockwell Automation, Inc.
Wes Wagnon of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Charles Luce, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC.'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Luce was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to submit with its moving papers verified factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support her claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff objected on the grounds of no verifications. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) As to the government contractor defense argument, defendant did not include this as grounds upon which it based its motion for summary judgment and/or summary adjudication in its notice of motion or separate statement. (CRC 3.1350.) Even if the court does consider defendant's argument, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it is entitled to prevail on the government contractor defense. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence demonstrating that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. By failing to submit with its moving papers verified factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support her claim for punitive damages, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff objected on the grounds of no verifications. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.). A revised order was signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)
